title: Second Great Awakening
description: Test your skills on the causes and effects of the Second Great Awakening

assessment_items.question_content:
> “When the churches are thus awakened and reformed, the reformation and salvation of sinners will follow, going through the same stages for conviction, repentance, and reformation. Their hearts will be broken down and changed. Very often the most abandoned profligates are among the subjects. Harlots, and drunkards, and infidels, and all sorts of abandoned characters are awakened and converted.”

> -Source: Charles G. Finney, “What A Revival of Religion Is,” *New York Evangelist,* 1834

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt are more closely aligned with which of the following broader historical developments?**


[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the Second Great Awakening
the temperance movement
the abolitionist movement
the penitentiary reform movement

assessment_items.question_content:
> “When the churches are thus awakened and reformed, the reformation and salvation of sinners will follow, going through the same stages for conviction, repentance, and reformation. Their hearts will be broken down and changed. Very often the most abandoned profligates are among the subjects. Harlots, and drunkards, and infidels, and all sorts of abandoned characters are awakened and converted.”

> -Source: Charles G. Finney, “What A Revival of Religion Is,” *New York Evangelist,* 1834

**The ideas expressed in the excerpt contributed most directly to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of the temperance movement, the abolition movement, and the suffrage movement
the decline in church membership across most Christian religions, including Methodists and Baptists
the rise in religious intolerance as Christianity grew and spread across the United States
the decline in political participation for white men, including voting rates and party membership

assessment_items.question_content:
> “When the churches are thus awakened and reformed, the reformation and salvation of sinners will follow, going through the same stages for conviction, repentance, and reformation. Their hearts will be broken down and changed. Very often the most abandoned profligates are among the subjects. Harlots, and drunkards, and infidels, and all sorts of abandoned characters are awakened and converted.”

> -Source: Charles G. Finney, “What A Revival of Religion Is,” *New York Evangelist,* 1834

**Which of the following best describes one similarity shared between the First Great Awakening and the Second Great Awakening?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a push for individual responsibility
a decline in female membership in the church
a denouncement of a democratic government
a rise in membership in the Church of Jesus Christ of Latter Day Saints

>-Source: Kelloggs & Comstock, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tent_revival_during_the_Second_Great_Awakening.jpg), 1849

**Which of the following most directly led to the experiences illustrated by the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: increased desire to experience religious conversion
increased government funding for religious institutions
rapid conversion of recent immigrants to Christianity
greater concern for social issues like alcoholism and slavery

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29be8265821310ea84d8aebbca921757.jpg)

>-Source: Kelloggs & Comstock, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tent_revival_during_the_Second_Great_Awakening.jpg), 1849

**Which of the following statements best summarizes what is depicted in the image?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a religious revival
a political rally
a social justice protest
a revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/29be8265821310ea84d8aebbca921757.jpg)

>-Source: Kelloggs & Comstock, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:Tent_revival_during_the_Second_Great_Awakening.jpg), 1849

**The situation depicted in the image contributed most immediately to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: women’s increased participation in reform movements
women seeking employment in factories to support their families
women adopting the “separate spheres” ideology
women joining political parties to influence policy

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a2227947979b900f84c01319f9a39e26.jpg)

 > -Source: J. Maze Burbank, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1839-meth.jpg), 1839

**The activity depicted in the image was most directly in response to which of the following developments?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: rise of deism and Enlightenment ideals
extension of suffrage to all white men
increased immigration from Ireland and Germany
change in gender roles after the Market Revolution

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a2227947979b900f84c01319f9a39e26.jpg)

 >-Source: J. Maze Burbank, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1839-meth.jpg), 1839

**The activity like that shown in the image contributed most directly to which of the following?**	

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: rise in church membership
increased westward expansion
extension of suffrage to women
creation of religious public schools

assessment_items.question_content:
![](${☣ LOCALPATH}/images/a2227947979b900f84c01319f9a39e26.jpg)

 >-Source: J. Maze Burbank, [Wikimedia Commons](https://commons.wikimedia.org/wiki/File:1839-meth.jpg), 1839

**The image most directly reflects which of the following trends of the early nineteenth century?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: the rise of Protestant evangelical fervor in new western states and territories
the expanding political participation of religious groups
the growth of nativism in response to increased immigration
the decline of children attending secular public schools

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Religious identity . . . allowed women to assert themselves, both in private and in public ways. It enabled them to rely on an authority beyond the world of men. . . . In contrast to the self-abnegation required of women in their domestic vocation, religious commitment required attention to one’s own thoughts, actions, and prospects. . . . No other avenue of self-expression besides religion at once offered women social approbation, the encouragement of male leaders (ministers), and, most important, the community of their peers.”

>-Source: Nancy Cott, *The Bonds of Womanhood: "Woman's Sphere" in New England, 1780-1835,* 1977

**Which of the following cultural and social shifts resulted most directly from the trends described in the excerpt?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: a rise of reform movements, like the temperance movement and the abolitionist movement
a decrease in utopian societies that were popular during the Era of Good Feelings
an increase in support for women taking political leadership positions in Congress
a decline in women’s participation in factory work in favor of staying home and caring for her family

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Religious identity . . . allowed women to assert themselves, both in private and in public ways. It enabled them to rely on an authority beyond the world of men. . . . In contrast to the self-abnegation required of women in their domestic vocation, religious commitment required attention to one’s own thoughts, actions, and prospects. . . . No other avenue of self-expression besides religion at once offered women social approbation, the encouragement of male leaders (ministers), and, most important, the community of their peers.”

>-Source: Nancy Cott, *The Bonds of Womanhood: "Woman's Sphere" in New England, 1780-1835,* 1977

**According to the passage, which of the following best explains the most important effect that the Second Great Awakening had on American women?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: It gave women an avenue to assert their individual agency.
It empowered women to search for employment.
It extended suffrage to all white women.
It changed women’s role within the household.

assessment_items.question_content:
>“Religious identity . . . allowed women to assert themselves, both in private and in public ways. It enabled them to rely on an authority beyond the world of men. . . . In contrast to the self-abnegation required of women in their domestic vocation, religious commitment required attention to one’s own thoughts, actions, and prospects. . . . No other avenue of self-expression besides religion at once offered women social approbation, the encouragement of male leaders (ministers), and, most important, the community of their peers.”

>-Source: Nancy Cott, *The Bonds of Womanhood: "Woman's Sphere" in New England, 1780-1835,* 1977

**The conditions described in the excerpt most directly contributed to which of the following?**

[[☃ radio 1]]

choices.content: women’s growing participation in antebellum reform movements
women refusing to join the movement for suffrage
women dividing by racial lines on the issue of slavery
women’s increased employment in factories
